todaysEvents.summary: Iran FM Araghchi departs Pakistan without meeting US envoys; Trump tells Witkoff and Kushner not to travel to Islamabad ('Too much time wasted on traveling'); second Islamabad round fully collapses. Araghchi: 'Have yet to see if US is truly serious about diplomacy' (Inquirer, NBC Live Blog, Apr 25).
todaysEvents.delta: Escalation: Both sides publicly stand down from diplomacy - ceasefire nominally in force but no active back-channel; tail risk rises to 50%

todaysEvents.summary: Trump orders US Navy to 'shoot and kill' any Iranian boat laying mines in Strait of Hormuz - 'There is to be no hesitation'; US military to heighten mine-removal operations (Al Jazeera, CNBC, WashPost, PBS, TIME, CBC, Apr 23). Iran continues Hormuz enforcement; US blockade intact.
todaysEvents.delta: Escalation: Explicit lethal ROE for IRGC small boats raises risk of inadvertent US-Iran naval exchange; new escalation vector alongside ongoing vessel seizures

todaysEvents.summary: Trump: 'no time frame' on Iran war, 'Don't rush me' (CNN, Apr 26); ceasefire nominally active; Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended 3 weeks (CBS News, Apr 25). Iran holds 3 Western commercial vessels (MSC Francesca, Epaminondas, Euphoria); Brent $105.33 confirmed Apr 24 close; tail risk 50%.
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral/Lateral: No new kinetic events today - Trump 'no time frame' signals no imminent US escalation; talks collapse has no immediate off-ramp

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Indirect back-channel reopens - Pakistan facilitates message exchange after talks collapse. Iran agrees on unified indirect proposal; vessels returned; ceasefire extended. Narrow (7%) - requires both sides to re-engage after publicly standing down. Note: even on deal, Pentagon told Congress mine-clearing takes ~6 months (PBS, Fox News); Avenger minesweepers en route from Japan; full commercial reopening delayed regardless of diplomacy. Brent retreats toward $92-98/bbl (not sub-$88) on deal; iTraxx tightens toward 180bp.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Talks-collapsed stalemate - second Islamabad round fails (Apr 25); back-channel technically open but both sides non-committal. US blockade and IRGC Hormuz enforcement co-exist; selective bilateral transit; periodic vessel seizures. Brent stabilises $95-115/bbl; JKM $17-22/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 200-220bp. Malaysia June supply gap remains live; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026. Dominant scenario (43%) - kinetic stalemate without resolution or collapse.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Talks collapse triggers ceasefire breakdown - no diplomatic pathway after Araghchi departs Pakistan and US envoys stand down (Apr 25). IRGC interprets diplomatic vacuum as green light for further escalation; US 'shoot and kill' ROE triggers incident with IRGC small boat; ceasefire formally terminated. Gulf-wide blockade activated; Kharg Island seizure or Aramco targeting brings GCC production under acute threat. Brent spikes $130-170/bbl; Malaysia June gap becomes July-August crisis. Tail at 50% (up from 40%) - both sides publicly walked away from diplomacy.
